plain_english_summary: Deterministic sensor compression SDK for IoT time-series and chemosense streams.
commercial_wedge: Reduce bandwidth and edge compute load for industrial IoT sensor streams. 17× mean compression on real public datasets with bounded-lossy fidelity guarantees and deterministic replay.
technical_wedge: Rust-core 8-primitive geometric codec with Python bindings. 17.16× mean CR across 11 real-public datasets, 27/27 destructive-test pass, byte-identical replay.
ideal_first_buyer: Industrial IoT platform team or edge telemetry vendor
deployment_model: SDK with Rust core and Python bindings
not_claimed: Universal compressor dominance (DS-12 competitor win, DS-11 blocked)
Direct Gorilla parity (Gorilla-proxy comparator is a simplified XOR+zlib proxy, not Facebook's production codec)
Unlimited stream length (65,536-sample hard cap via 2-byte header; ~16 min at 60 Hz)
NaN/Inf tolerance (non-finite inputs cause codec failure)

benchmark_methodology.cr_note: Against float32 baselines, ratios are approximately half
benchmark_methodology.gorilla_proxy: Simplified ~25-line XOR-delta + zlib proxy inspired by Facebook Gorilla; not the production codec
benchmark_methodology.lossy_vs_lossless: ZPE-IoT is bounded-lossy; baselines (zstd, LZ4, zlib, Gorilla-proxy) are lossless

family_position: Product candidate in the Zer0pa deterministic encoding family. ZPE-IMC is the umbrella integration layer.
